title: Crawl Graphs, Frontier Strategy, Visited State, Depth, and Determinism · Language Clinic: Deterministic Frontier Evidence
summary: URLs are marked visited after download, duplicates race into the queue, depth changes with concurrency, and restart cannot explain which parent discovered a page. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the deterministic frontier evidence increment in deterministic crawl-frontier state machine. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Use stable tie-breakers and seeded fixtures so the same admitted graph yields a reproducible decision and report order.
Record first and alternate parents, discovery depth, relation, priority, and scope decision without making completion order define depth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-deterministic-frontier-evidence — Use stable tie-breakers and seeded fixtures so the same admitted graph yields a reproducible decision and report order.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-graph-frontier-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-graph-frontier-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_crawl_graph_frontier_3(admitted_urls: int = 64, reserved_urls: int = 64, completed_urls: int = 58, rejected_urls: int = 6, duplicate_schedules: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if admitted_urls < 1 or reserved_urls < 0:
        return False, reserved_urls, "frontier-count"
    if reserved_urls != admitted_urls:
        return False, reserved_urls, "reservation-gap"
    if completed_urls + rejected_urls != reserved_urls:
        return False, completed_urls, "state-reconciliation"
    if duplicate_schedules > 0:
        return False, duplicate_schedules, "duplicate-schedule"
    assert completed_urls + rejected_urls == reserved_urls and duplicate_schedules == 0
    return True, completed_urls, "deterministic-crawl-frontier"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python crawler decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected authorization, URL, response, tree, record, frontier, browser, report, or recovery case, then state which network, owner, robots, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, accessibility, privacy, security, legal, load, schedule, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-deterministic-frontier-evidence-1aayps
# Competency: scraper-deterministic-frontier-evidence.
# Case 1912: an authorized nonprofit content audit.
# Operating constraint: personal data must remain minimized and redacted.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def evidence_scraper_deterministic_frontier_evidence_1aayps(admitted_urls: int = 64, reserved_urls: int = 64, completed_urls: int = 58, rejected_urls: int = 6, duplicate_schedules: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1aayps = "1aayps-spyaa1-1912"
    assert evidence_variant_1aayps
    evidence_axes_1aayps = (
        "authorization1aayps",
        "url1912",
        "responsespyaa1",
        "tree1aayps1912",
        "frontier19121aayps",
        "reportspyaa11912",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1aayps)) == 6
    if admitted_urls < 1 or reserved_urls < 0:
        return False, reserved_urls, "frontier-count"
    if reserved_urls != admitted_urls:
        return False, reserved_urls, "reservation-gap"
    if completed_urls + rejected_urls != reserved_urls:
        return False, completed_urls, "state-reconciliation"
    if duplicate_schedules > 0:
        return False, duplicate_schedules, "duplicate-schedule"
    assert completed_urls + rejected_urls == reserved_urls and duplicate_schedules == 0
    return True, completed_urls, "deterministic-crawl-frontier"
